title: Level 355
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Syne+Mono&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto+Condensed:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Share+Tech+Mono&display=swap');}} {{Blurbackground|file=FireBG.jpg}} {{CSS|content= .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5 { Font-family: Syne Mono; Padding: 5px; Text-align: center; text-shadow: 0px 0px 2px 10px grey, 0px 9px 4px 12px black; border: groove 3px #000000; border-radius: 15px 10px 10px 15px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px 5px #ff5000, 0px 0px 5px 500px rgba(0, 0, 0,0); text-shadow: 0px 0px 2px 10px grey, 0px 9px 4px 12px black; background-color: rgba(105, 42, 25,0.6); } :root { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(105, 42, 25, 0); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: linear-gradient(to top, #693122, rgba(224, 78, 39, 0.5)); --theme-page-text-color: #ffffff; --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-link-color: #ff3500; --theme-accent-label-color: linear-gradient(to top, white, rgba(0,0,255,1)); --theme-border-color: #ff5000; }} {{CSS|content= .page-side-tool { border: 1px solid white; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px #ff5000; } .page { margin-top: 10px; padding: 5px; position: relative; border: groove 3px #000000; border-radius: 15px 10px 10px 15px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px 5px #ff5000, 0px 0px 5px 500px rgba(0, 0, 0,0); text-shadow: 0px 0px 2px 10px grey, 0px 9px 4px 12px black; background-color: rgba(105, 42, 25,0.6); }}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 355: "Ashes, Ashes"}} {{Alt Class|ent=0|env=4|ext=3|no=355}}
'''Level 355''' is the 356th level of the Backrooms. ----
{{Blurimage|file=Found355.jpg|caption=Found footage of a wanderer walking through the level.|width=500px|align=center}}
---- == Description == '''Level 355''', also known colloquially as '''"Ashes, Ashes,"''' takes the appearance of a desolate forest of dead trees, with signs of burning. The woodland is presumably many millions of kilometers in diameter, and the topography while mostly flat, can dip and rise in various places. The level's ambient temperature is at a constant 4° Celsius (40° Fahrenheit). Wanderers enter the level within 600 kilometers (372 miles) of the level's center, which allows equipped wanderers to locate each other upon entry. The entire level is shrouded in darkness besides the faint glow of distant wildfires and a few glowing cinders in the sky. Entities, nor objects, have ever been confirmed to appear here. The forests are comprised of thin, tall trees, that do not quite match any species of tree from the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. The level lacks a significant day/night cycle due to the lack of a sun, resulting in permanent night. While many wanderers have reported seeing stars in the night sky, they were confirmed to be floating, burning soot. The level is covered in a thin layer of ash, and the forest is heavily damaged, appearing scorched. The sky is almost always covered in smoke clouds, as well as a thick smog which covers most of the level's surface. The level's notorious amounts of air pollutants make menial tasks such as walking, climbing, or even breathing difficult to do properly. ----
{{Blurimage|file=Girthybranch.png|align=left|caption=Another found image of Level 355.|width=500px|align=center}}
---- == Burntwood Trees == The trees on the level, known as ''Burntwood trees/Lignis ciniscinis'', are extremely durable to heat; however, appear charred on the surface. The trees look sickly, with a few dry leaves and branches placed randomly across the trunk. Their height averages at 5-10 meters, and they are weak to force, causing some to collapse when pressure is applied. Strangely, when wind blows through the trees, the sound of crackling fire can be heard. This sound is described to feel like a "burning" memory of the past, and is reported to cause a wide range of emotions - mainly sadness, longing, and nostalgia. They also emit a strong, acrid scent, described to smell sooty and hazy. Sometimes, contact with the trees can bring back flashbacks, commonly related to family. The ground of the level has been known to collapse underneath people's feet, and after some investigation, the trees seem to be to blame. Burntwoods do not have large roots like most trees in the Frontrooms. Instead, their roots are flimsy, and they are only about 30 centimeters on average. Underneath the ground, there can be large cave systems, meaning that the ground can fall a great height before stopping. If the trees had regular, sturdy roots, this would be a non-issue. The branches of the trees have become an important asset to psychologists in the Backrooms, and they are commonly sold with the name of Wish Sticks. Possession of these sticks can help a person heal their mental wounds, and is especially effective for wanderers whose loved ones have died. These effects can only be harnessed after the twigs are sawed off and have no effect while they are still attached to the tree. While it does work for all, people with deteriorated mental conditions exhibit far worse side effects as a result of even touching the branches. The branches also are resistant to fire, and when presented with heat, they appear to jitter away. After close examination, the molecules in the core of the Wish Sticks have been revealed to be variations of carbon, coal, and oxidized copper. This revelation has sparked many theories involving Level 355, suggesting a large forest fire could have engulfed the finite forest, resulting in its burnt condition. ----
{{Blurimage|file=Ashesashesfirefirewhatwhat.jpg|align=right|caption=An image of Fake Fire.|width=500px}}
---- == Fake Fires == A common phenomenon in Level 355 is referred to as Fake Fire, and appears to be sections of flame on the forest's ground. Fake Fire has two main anomalous properties: it seems frozen in time, and it does not burn. These regions of fire look as though they are holographs, and do not move or spread on their own. The holographs are also semi-transparent and cause no physical harm, meaning it is safe to walk through them. Like Burntwood trees, they do cause a variety of mental syndromes - mainly fury, fear, and the feeling of pain. Any interaction with Fake Fire does not change it, so it can not be collected or experimented on. Fake Fire is responsible for most of the smoke and smog in the level. The only thing Fake Fire seems to react to is the dead. Dead organic matter, when interacting with fake fire, gets dissolved into ashes in mere seconds. These interactions usually happen when a tree finally lives out its life cycle and dies. At this moment, any nearby Fake Fire would get drawn to the tree and engulf it. Unlike real fire (and by extension the forest fires that can appear on the level), Fake Fire does not produce heat. In fact, when interacting with dead matter, its ambient temperatures can be as low as 19° Celsius (-2.2° Fahrenheit). Fake Fire in this stage glows a faint pink, and as its temperature rises, its hue shifts upwards. These blazes can be tricky to differentiate from wildfires, but there are some ways to identify them from each other. Easy ways to tell are the size; holographic flames are only dozens of meters, while the wildfires are dozens of kilometers. Another way is by testing the temperature of the two types of fires, however it is not recommended to do this since it could cause one to get burnt. == Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === * You can enter Level 355 by directly no-clipping into an open fire on most levels in the Backrooms. * Starting a fire in woodsy levels such as [[Level 45]] or [[Level 382]] can directly transition into Level 355. * Unconfirmed claims state that certain ''tragedies'' can lead here under the right circumstances. === Exits === * Spending more than 10 minutes engulfed in Fake Fire will take one to [[Level 106]]. * No-clipping into the largest trees, while it may take many tries, can lead back to [[Level 45]]. * No-clipping in the cave systems underground can lead to [[Level 8]], or [[Level 8.5]]. * Wandering far enough will eventually lead to [[Level 630]].

|body= {{Underline|ffffff}}
5/4/2021 A team of M.E.G. agents searching for wanderers in need of rescue in Level 355 recently came across a cave entrance deep in a valley. This never-before seen find let scientists see what was under the burnt forest for the first time. The rocks were made of limestone, but unusually weren't moist or damp. The entire cave was without any moisture whatsoever. Stalactites and stalagmites were also hardly present either. The cave had evidence of a wanderer(s) living within its lowest cellar. Camping supplies were found placed neatly on the floor, as well as a spare battery pack with 7 batteries left inside. A rock had tally marks etched in the sides, supposedly to count the days spent inside. Disturbingly, the deepest foreign object in the cave was puddles of dried blood next to a cave-in of rock and ash. Further updates pending.

|final=
'''You didn't care for the forest, and know you can't care for your daughter.'''

Transcript: Party 5 - 7:43:52

3: Ughhhh... all I can see is ashes and fires and ashes and ashes and ashes and... 2: Elias, please shut up! You've said this a million times! Please just focus on our mission. 3: But, do you really think we'll find someone? I doubt it. 1: I don't know, 355's 2,900 kilometers wide. We'll be at the wrong place at the right time, and we'll miss someone. 3: Topographical change 2 o'clock. 4: Woah, that is a big crater. 3: Crater? That's definitely a valley. 1: Too dark to tell. Lets keep moving. 3: Report that down here, the ground is a different color. It's slightly redder.
...
3: Yuck! It smells like smoke. 2: No shit, Sherlock! It always smells like smoke here. 3: No no, it smells a lot more pungent, it's stuck in my nose. 4: You're right. We're probably within a mile of a fake fire. 2: Translate? 4: Yeah, about a kilometer and a half from it. It- 2: Hold on, guys do you see that? 3: Looks like, a hole. 1: Oh my god. 3: Wait, report mission halted. 4: It's a cave opening. Maybe we can figure out why the ground is so unstable. 2: And maybe stop it all together. 3: Yeah, sure, in another couple of years. Until then, all I can see is ashes and fires and ashes and fires and ashes and fires and...

'''Don't play with fire...'''
|final=
'''Don't play with fire...'''
|body= {{Underline|ffffff}}
We had been walking through the forest for hours, or it seemed like it. The sounds of the city had died off, and the only thing that surrounded us was nature. I looked towards Ellie, who was swatting off a group of gnats, her face dull and expressionless. Sometimes, it looked like all the joy had been sucked out of her. Small talk would help, perhaps? I thought of the first sentence that came to mind.
“Well, it’s pretty hot today, right Ellie?”
She turned back at me, took a sudden glance, and quickly turned away. That did not go well. I knew she would be upset that we had to leave Albany, but there was something else going on. I should try to comfort her, maybe… I walked towards her, patted her shoulder, and looked at the morning sky.
“It’s a really nice day for camping, don’t ya think? Really nice. Um, do you remember, when Mom was here, we’d have - wait, sorry, I mean…” “Can you please be quiet, Dad! We’ve been walking here for hours, I just want to go home! If Mom was still here, we wouldn’t have to do this! We wouldn’t be broke!”
She quickly covered her mouth, and then looked away. She looked down at the ground, and slowed down her pace until me and her were close together. I put my hand around her, and this time, she didn’t push it back.
“We’re just trying something new, Ellie. Besides, I could always tell you scary stories. It’s about to be Halloween, right?”
She laughed for the first time today, and I smiled back at her.
“It can’t be Halloween, it’s July, Dad!”
I knew. Soon, we had come to a vast field of grass, with a gleaming, blue river running through it. I took off my muddied backpack and pulled out an unopened tent. Ellie had laid down adjacent to the river, taking in the shade of a nearby tree. I began to unfold the plastic tent. The scent of roses drifted through the air, vivid flowers blooming from the grass. It wasn’t our home in New York, but it felt right. As I unzipped the tent’s packaging, Ellie started looking through the bag.
“Hey, Ellie, while you’re there, could you make the fireplace? The seats are folded up, just look in the back pocket.”
She looked back at me, the smile on her face melting away. She sighed.
“We just came here! But, I have no choice, do I?” “Wait, wait, it’s okay if you don’t wan…”
She already began making it, and I knew she thought that I couldn’t, in my present state. She wasn’t wrong. My back still ached with each movement, but I tried to stay strong. I had to, for Ellie. I was the only person, the only parent, she had left. Soon, the moon came out, and we both huddled near the unfinished fire. She was carrying the firewood to our little nook in the shade, and she came back with her hand crooked. I looked at it, and saw a tiny splinter through her pinky, with droplets of blood dripping out. She tried rubbing the wound, but it only pained more.
“Ow…”
I reached in towards her, attempting to see the gash, but she quickly moved back. I saw her push the splinter into her finger, and I leaned towards her in horror, talking in a slow voice.
“Ellie, you are trying to do it again, aren’t you? It’s okay. Here, let me help take that piece of wood ou…” “No! No, I don’t need your help.”
Tears streamed down her eyes, she pushed my chair backwards, and I fell. My spine clashed into the ground, and it felt like my body had twisted.
“Go! Go away, Dad” “Ellie, please! Calm down!”
I attempted to reach back up to her, but she forced me down. I jumped up, and her chair staggered, the firewood slipping out of her hands and onto her legs. She screamed in pain, her face swarming with tears.
“I hate you! I hate all of this! I want to go back home to my friends! You had lost your job just to make me sad, didn't you! I wish Mom had survived the crash instead of you!”
The forest turned quiet. Her face became covered with dark red, and she drew her hand around me, sorrow in her eyes. I knew she didn’t mean it, she couldn’t have meant it. Did she mean it? She may have been correct.
“You know, I got a gift for you…” “What?” “Marshmallows, I remember that you like them. I put them in the tent, come on, we can roast them. They taste really good if you make them brown.” “Dad… I'm sorry”
I waved it off, but my eyes were still filled with water. I had to look strong, even if I wasn't.
“It’s okay, let’s not think about it anymore. It is in the past.”
She walked away to get the marshmallows… She was right about my wife. Before the crash, we used to be the picture perfect family, the ones everyone envied. Our income was high, our house was large enough to fit us all, and most importantly, we loved each other. Now, all three of those statements would be lies. After my wife died, I felt alone, lost, like I couldn’t do anything. My mental health reflected into my financial life, and soon, I was fired from my job. We had to sell our damn house just to pay for the debt! And, Ellie was different, restless, afraid, depressed. Who could have blamed her? She became my caretaker when I was supposed to be hers. I couldn’t care for my wife, and I couldn’t care for my kid. I can’t care for nothing. The campfire began to become brighter, the particles of flame rising higher up into the air, smoke covering the night sky. I hadn’t noticed it before, but now it was far too hot. As an instinct, I swiftly scavenged the floor for a bucket of water, but all the liquid had spilled from the fallen firewood. The flames rose into the sky, and soon, the fire had become taller than the tent. Suddenly, the tree above me caught on fire, and it crashed onto the ground, creating a barrier between me and the tent. The ground began to burn, and the chaos spread closer and closer towards me. I attempted to crawl back, but my mind kept me stuck. Ellie immediately ripped open the flaps of the tents, the fire circling her. She called for my help, and I tried to run towards her, but another tree had fallen down onto the dry ground.
“Ellie, go!” I cried out, but she was frozen. “RUN!”
Something grasped onto me, and pulled me towards the river.
“No, NO!” I stuttered. “Ellie!”
But it was too late, I was pulled into the stream, the frigid water taking me towards safety. I watched as the red vanished, and only gray remained.
...
I reached out towards a rock, hoisting my body out of the dry river, slowly sliding myself up. Smoke blew through the air, ash taking up my vision. The forest was not the same, the trees were burnt and dead. I wasn't in New York anymore, I was somewhere… different. I began to walk through the woods, hearing the crackling of a fireplace whistle in the wind. I turned around, and saw splotches of. Fire... Fire! The campfire had burned down the entire forest, I had destroyed the land, and I created this place. I couldn't care for my wife, for the forest, for Ellie. Ellie, I had almost forgotten about her! I ran between the trees, avoiding the fire, trying to make it back to camp. The river that had taken me here was gone and dried up, but I could see familiar trees and plants. All of a sudden, I smelled the scent of toasted marshmallows. I looked through the myriad of trees, and saw the tent. I closed my eyes, and felt someone grasp my hands. The fire took her here just like it did with me! Ellie was still…
“Alive! Eleanor, you're still here, you're still... safe?”
But she continued to hold my hands, crushing them, my fingers in pain. I tried to let go, but I couldn't. This wasn't Ellie, this was someone else. The person let go of me, and another voice penetrated my mind. The voice of Fire.
“You are a monster... to the forest and your child.”
She let go, and I opened my eyes, watching Ellie fall backwards and turn to ash, the fire succumbing her. The fire became still, frozen in time, my tears falling through it. I put my hand into the fire. It didn't burn. I wish it did. '''''
They will forever be lost in the whispers of the wind.
''''' '''''
The daughter is gone and the father has sinned.
'''''
